findings.title: Token application split across CSS and inline styles
findings.detail: The body element sets background, color, and fontFamily both in globals.css (via the body rule) and redundantly via inline styles in layout.tsx. This creates two places that must stay in sync. If the CSS body rule is the canonical token application point, the inline style object on the body tag is redundant and adds confusion about which takes precedence.
findings.fix: Remove the inline style object from the body in layout.tsx. Let globals.css body { background: var(--color-bg); color: var(--color-text); font-family: var(--font-nunito); } be the single application point. The body className already provides structural Tailwind utilities (min-h-full flex flex-col), which is the correct separation.

summary: Architecture is sound — Server Component used correctly, no client/server boundary violations, App Router conventions followed; one minor DRY issue with token application duplicated between CSS and inline styles.
